business.headline: Gold is rising slowly: Should you expect a big move soon?Gold has been moving up, but quietly. There's no sudden surge, just a gradual rise. So the big question is—does this signal a strong rally ahead or more sideways movement?

IMF warns Asia may face sharper hit from Middle East energy shockAsia relies heavily on imported oil and gas, particularly from the Middle East. This makes it more vulnerable when supply disruptions or price spikes occur.

Gold, equities or debt? Expert says no asset stays on top for longIf you're waiting to invest in the "best" asset, you may be chasing a moving target. An expert explains why gold, equities and debt keep rotating as top performers over time.

NPS, EPF or mutual funds: Where should you invest Rs 5,000 for 10 yearsYou set aside Rs 5,000 every month with one simple goal, i.e., to build something meaningful over 10 years. But the real question is where to invest it. NPS, EPF or mutual funds? Let's have a closer look.

Sensex slips 123 points, Nifty ends below 24,200 as markets erase early gainsThe BSE Sensex closed at 77,988.68, down 122.56 points or 0.16%. The NSE Nifty 50 settled at 24,196.75, lower by 34.55 points or 0.14%.

Sensex falls 1,000 points after early gains: What should investors do?The BSE Sensex moved within a wide range of 1,055.39 points, touching a high of 78,730.32 and a low of 77,674.93. The NSE Nifty 50 also saw similar movement, trading in a range of 298.15 points between a high of 24,400.95 and a low of 24,102.80.

Income below exemption limit? These 3 transactions make ITR filing compulsoryNo taxable income doesn't always mean no ITR. Certain transactions can bring you under the tax net, even if your earnings are below the basic limit.

From Rs 50 to Rs 200: How you can save up to Rs 1.05 lakh with meal vouchersWhat if your daily lunch could help you save tax? With the limit rising from Rs 50 to Rs 200 per meal, meal vouchers are suddenly becoming a powerful tax-saving tool for salaried employees.

DA hike has been delayed, but will you get it this year?If you've been waiting for the DA hike announcement, you might have noticed the silence this time. It hasn't come when expected, but does that mean trouble, or just a delay?

Workforce shrinks, pay hikes normalise: How TCS is reshaping hiring amid AI boomTCS has restored its salary cycle, but with more selective hiring and differentiated pay, signalling a shift towards skills and performance as the key drivers of workforce growth.

Mark Mobius dies at 89: The investor who took global money to emerging marketsMark Mobius built his reputation by investing in emerging markets that many investors once avoided due to political risk, weak governance and currency volatility. His approach helped draw billions of dollars into these regions.

8th Pay Commission: Will Rs 69,000 minimum pay actually happen?The proposal, submitted by the staff side of the National Council–Joint Consultative Machinery, has quickly grabbed attention for the scale of the increase it seeks.
Sensex opens 400 points higher, Nifty above 24,300; IT stocks upThe S&P BSE Sensex added 550.07 points to 78,661.31, while the NSE Nifty50 was up by 158.10 points to 24,389.40 as of 9:27 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market continue its rally or fall today?Early indicators suggest a positive start. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 24,317.5 at 7:59 am, pointing to an opening above Wednesday's close of 24,231.3 for the Nifty 50.

TCS filing made easier: Income Tax Dept brings in new Form 143, replacing 27EQThe move is part of a wider effort to simplify tax processes under the updated income-tax framework and reduce errors in filing.

Indian delegation to visit US from April 20 as trade talks resumeAn Indian delegation led by the chief negotiator will visit the US from April 20. This will be the first in-person meeting between the two sides in about three to four months.
Sensex ends 1,264 points higher, Nifty above 24,200; IT stocks jumpThe S&P BSE Sensex jumped 1,263.67 points to close at 78,111.24, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 38.65 points to end at 24,231.30.

Is India's gold loan boom quietly building a default risk?Gold loans have grown nearly 3.8 times since March 2022. Their share in the retail credit market has also increased sharply, rising from 5.9% to 11.1% by December 2025. This makes gold loans the second-largest retail loan category after home loans.

DA hike update: Central government staff to protest on April 16Weeks after it was expected, the DA hike for January 2026 is still pending. With no update from the government, central government employee body is preparing to protest on April 16.

Zero tax to pay? 3 big reasons why you should still file your ITRIf your income is low and your taxes come to zero, you might think filing an ITR is not needed. But skipping it could mean missing out on some useful benefits that go beyond just paying tax.

entertainment.headline: Avengers: Doomsday trailer: Thor attacks Stormbreaker, Doom blocks him Marvel Studios revealed the first trailer for Avengers: Doomsday at CinemaCon 2026, generating huge excitement among fans. The film promises intense battles, a multiverse storyline, and the return of beloved characters along with new recruits.

I try not to judge my characters: Kritika Kamra returns in crime drama Matka King Kritika Kamra prefers not to be boxed in "good girl" and "bad girl" roles, and says it doesn't matter if her characters are likeable to all. The actor returns in the crime drama series Matka King.

No one better to play Lord Rama: Ramayana producer's praise for Ranbir Kapoor Ramayana producer Namit Malhotra heaped praise on Ranbir Kapoor, calling him the finest actor of our generation. He also backed his casting as Lord Rama in Ramayana.

Ramayana, Adipurush, Mahadev: Our screen gods look straight out of the gym lately From films to soaps, Indian pop culture is increasingly portraying male gods from Ramayana, Mahabharata and Puranas as gym-toned hunks who could give Chris Hemsworth's Thor a run for his money.

RGV: Dhurandhar 2 shows Dawood Ibrahim incorrectly, he is inactive by choice Director Ram Gopal Varma questioned the portrayal of gangster Dawood Ibrahim in Dhurandhar 2. While praising the film, he suggested Dawood may have stepped away from crime years ago.

Babil Khan shares how Irrfan cared for wife during cancer, posts throwback pics Actor Babil Khan shared a heartfelt memory of late father Irrfan caring for his wife during his cancer battle. The celebrated actor died in 2020 at 53.

Gaurav Gera: Aditya Dhar handles pressure with ease, has a no-chaos work style Gaurav Gera praised Dhurandhar director Aditya Dhar's calm and composed nature while working on the blockbuster film. He shared how Dhar's evolved mindset and easygoing approach on set left a lasting impression on him.

Victoria Beckham breaks silence, avoids Brooklyn's mention in cryptic comment The Beckham family drama just won't die down. Victoria Beckham has now subtly weighed in on her fallout with son Brooklyn in a new interview without naming him.
Meghan Markle joins MasterChef Australia as guest judge. Watch promo Meghan Markle will appear as a guest judge on MasterChef Australia Season 18, marking her return to Australia after 2018.

Kangana Ranaut recalls fallout with ex: Was called a witch, accused of black magic Kangana Ranaut recently spoke about a difficult phase in her late 20s when she faced severe personal attacks and was falsely accused of practising black magic. She described the experience as a witch-hunt.

Sai Tamhankar: That era, when women were just ornamental, is gone Sai Tamhankar, in an exclusive interaction with India Today, reflected on the positive change in the portrayal of women in Indian cinema across genres.

Miss World? Me? Priyanka Chopra's throwback post recalling life, career goes viral Priyanka Chopra Jonas revisited her journey from childhood dreams to global stardom, reflecting on her work in films, entrepreneurship, motherhood and her move from India to Hollywood. She will be seen next in SS Rajamouli's Varanasi.

She's an electric scene partner: Laetitia Hollard on working with Katherine LaNasa Laetitia Hollard found a real-life mentor in The Pitt co-star Kaherine LaNasa. The actor, who plays nurse Emma Nolan in the Emmy-winning medical drama, opened up about the on-set bond that mirrored her character's own journey.

Actor Daisy Shah recalls being 'touched badly' by a South filmmaker Daisy Shah recalled an uncomfortable casting incident early in her career, involving inappropriate contact by a filmmaker, but chose not to name him.

Kangana Ranaut thinks Karan Johar is 'not a bad person, just superficial' Kangana Ranaut revisited her complicated equation with Karan Johar, calling him "flaky." She also looked back at an early moment of validation from Salman Khan.

Udta Teer: Ayushmann Khurrana, Sara Ali Khan's spy comedy gets a release date Ayushmann Khurrana and Sara Ali Khan are teaming up for the spy comedy Udta Teer. The film, directed by Akash A Kaushik, has locked in a release date.

Jana Nayagan leak: Freelance editor stole footage from studio, 3 arrested The Tamil Nadu Cyber Crime Wing has arrested three key suspects involved in the online leak of Jana Nayagan. This move comes amid heightened efforts to curb illegal distribution and protect the film industry from such breaches.

Exclusive: No imitating Michael, say Jaafar Jackson and director Antoine Fuqua Actor Jaafar Jackson and director Antoine Fuqua have said that the upcoming musical, Michael, does not intend to imitate Michael Jackson.

Kangana Ranaut supports 'top-most actress' Deepika Padukone's 8-hour work demand Kangana Ranaut weighed in on the debate sparked by Deepika Padukone's reported stance on an eight-hour workday, backing her right to set boundaries at this stage in her career.

general news.headline: Nida Khan, on the run, is not HR head but telecaller at TCS Nashik unit: SourcesSources told Business Today, India Today's sister site, that Nida Khan is not the HR head but a telecaller at the TCS Nashik BPO unit. The unit has been at the centre of a major scandal involving sex harassment and forced religious conversion.

Trump claims Iran agrees to hand over enriched uranium, says deal very closeTrump says US and Iran are close to a deal after six weeks of conflict, with talks likely this weekend, ceasefire uncertain, and warning that fighting could resume if negotiations fail.

FIR against Vedanta chairman Anil Agarwal after Chhattisgarh boiler blast kills 20An FIR has been filed against Vedanta chairman Anil Agarwal and other officials after a boiler blast at a Chhattisgarh power plant killed 20 workers and injured several others.

Kohli likes South African influencer's photo, sparks hilarious 'algorithm' buzzIPL 2026: A simple Instagram like by Virat Kohli sparked a viral "algorithm" meme fest online. Fans quickly revived his old explanation, turning yet another social media moment into light-hearted humour.

People paid Rs 8 crore to run, lift at Hyrox Bengaluru event that has India talkingAround 9,000 people gathered at the Bangalore International Exhibition Centre on the weekend to do workouts one could easily do in a park or gym. Each person paid Hyrox over Rs 9,000 for that. It has sparked a debate over whether Hyrox workouts are merely a status symbol or a shift in how India approaches fitness.

Live: DMK warns to move court if delimitation bill is passed in ParliamentSpecial Parliament Session Live Updates: High-voltage debate on women's reservation and delimitation on Thursday saw PM Modi pitch the quota as a long-overdue democratic reform with assurances of fairness, while Amit Shah detailed the 850-seat framework and insisted no state will lose representation despite a shift in proportional share. On the other side, Priyanka Gandhi backed 33% reservation but slammed its linkage with delimitation, questioning intent, timing, and demanding immediate implementation with sub-quotas, as the Opposition warned of political motives and alleged regional imbalance.

Major flight disruption coming? Europe has just 6 weeks of ATF leftAmid the ongoing Iran war, the chief of the International Energy Agency (IEA) has warned that Europe has only six weeks of jet fuel remaining. With summer being the peak season for travel in Europe, the fuel shortage might hit air travel hard.

IPL Play of the Day: Ice-cold Shreyas Iyer proves he is the new King of ChasesMI vs PBKS: Shreyas Iyer downed Mumbai Indians with a terrific innings of 66 off 35 balls. This was the third straight match where Shreyas hit a fifty in the chase, helping the team remain unbeaten this season.

Ramayana, Adipurush, Mahadev: Our screen gods look straight out of the gym latelyFrom films to soaps, Indian pop culture is increasingly portraying male gods from Ramayana, Mahabharata and Puranas as gym-toned hunks who could give Chris Hemsworth's Thor a run for his money.

I might go: Trump signals possible Pak visit if Iran deal is signed in IslamabadTrump hints at a possible Pakistan visit if a deal with Iran is signed in Islamabad, while stressing that Tehran must not acquire nuclear weapons and praising Pakistan's constructive role in facilitating dialogue between Washington and Tehran.

Why didn't they move after siren? MLA's son rams Thar into bike, blames ridersAn MLA's son in Madhya Pradesh rammed his SUV into a two-wheeler and pedestrians, and then blamed them for not clearing the way despite his siren.

Bhooth Bangla Review and Release Live: A laugh riot, say early reviewsBhooth Bangla Movie Review and Release Live Updates: Bhooth Bangla marks actor Akshay Kumar and director Priyadarshan's reunion after 14 years. The film brings back the horror-comedy genre with a star-studded cast.

No coaching, just internet and books: Gurgaon teen scores 100% in CBSE, eyes JEE mainTanay Srivastav's perfect 100% in CBSE Class 10 Board Exams 2026 sets a new benchmark. His success reflects the power of self-study, discipline, and conceptual clarity. A well-rounded achiever, he inspires CBSE and JEE aspirants, proving consistency and curiosity drive success.

In 37 minutes, PM turned women's quota debate into a masterclass in messagingPrime Minister Narendra Modi steered a high-stakes Lok Sabha debate on the women's quota, blending optics with sharp political messaging as the Opposition signalled resistance through notes, nudges and coordinated responses on the floor.

Trouble in MI? Jasprit Bumrah's reaction to Hardik Pandya's captaincy call goes viralThings don't seem to be good inside the Mumbai Indians camp as a viral video shows Jasprit Bumrah being unhappy at the call made by Hardik Pandya during the loss to Punjab Kings at the Wankhede Stadium on Thursday, April 16.

Seasoned diplomat: Trump praises ex-India envoy on Delhi Lt Governor appointmentSandhu, a former Indian Ambassador to the United States, played a key role during a crucial phase in India-US relations marked by increased strategic cooperation, defence partnerships and economic engagement.

Truth about Pakistan, Munir revealed: Iran visit video spills beansPakistan army chief Asim Munir's Tehran visit has once again revealed who holds power in Pakistan. While Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi stood back, Munir was the first person to de-board the flight in Tehran and was greeted by Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi.

Live: Lebanon accuses Israel of ceasefire violations, calls it acts of aggressionUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: A 10-day truce came into effect in Lebanon on Friday, aimed at pausing fighting between Israel and the Hezbollah militant group and supporting efforts to extend a broader ceasefire involving Iran, US and Israel after weeks of intense conflict. However, Lebanon's army said Israel carried out "acts of aggression" that it described as violations of the ceasefire, which took effect at midnight local time. However, the situation on the ground remains uncertain, with Hezbollah indicating that its participation in any truce depends on Israel halting all forms of hostilities. Meanwhile, the humanitarian toll continues to rise, with Lebanon's National News Agency reporting 2,196 deaths and 7,185 injuries from ongoing Israeli attacks. Additionally, US President Donald Trump said a deal to end the war with Iran was "very close" and suggested talks could resume in Islamabad as early as this weekend. Follow indiatoday.in for live updates.

RGV: Dhurandhar 2 shows Dawood Ibrahim incorrectly, he is inactive by choiceDirector Ram Gopal Varma questioned the portrayal of gangster Dawood Ibrahim in Dhurandhar 2. While praising the film, he suggested Dawood may have stepped away from crime years ago.

Vinod Kambli is fine: Wife addresses health speculationFormer cricketer Vinod Kambli's wife, Andrea Hewitt, has dismissed speculation around his health, stating that the former India batter is 'fine'. Her statement comes amid reports suggesting Kambli was battling serious medical issues and remained at risk of a brain stroke.

How Pete Hegseth brought Pulp Fiction Bible verse to PentagonAt a Pentagon prayer service, President Trump's Secretary of War, Pete Hegseth, recited what sounded like scripture, but was largely lifted from Pulp Fiction. Hegseth's use of the fictitious Bible lines, delivered in the movie by Samuel L Jackson, blurred the line between faith and fiction.

Flag bearer for feminism: Kangana Ranaut praises PM during women's quota debateKangana Ranaut praised PM Modi as the "flag bearer of feminism" in Lok Sabha, backing women's quota reforms and attacking Congress for delaying the issue for decades while in power.

Chanakya would've been shocked: Priyanka Gandhi to Amit Shah over women's quotaCongress's Wayanad MP Priyanka Gandhi Vadra took a 'Chanakya' swipe at Union Home Minister Amit Shah during the women's quota debate. Her remark saw Union Home Minister Amit Shah smiling.

Innocent, falsely implicated: TCS Nashik case accused's mother to India TodayTabassum Sheikh, mother of Danish Sheikh, co-accused in the TCS Nashik sex harassment and forced conversion case, denied all allegations against her son. "He is a very good person," she insisted.

Fans quickly revived his old explanation, turning yet another social media moment into light-hearted humour.

Arshdeep picked up three wickets in the win against MI.

Trouble in MI? Jasprit Bumrah's reaction to Hardik Pandya's captaincy call goes viral Things don't seem to be good inside the Mumbai Indians camp as a viral video shows Jasprit Bumrah being unhappy at the call made by Hardik Pandya during the loss to Punjab Kings at the Wankhede Stadium on Thursday, April 16.

Vinod Kambli is fine: Wife addresses health speculation Former cricketer Vinod Kambli's wife, Andrea Hewitt, has dismissed speculation around his health, stating that the former India batter is 'fine'. Her statement comes amid reports suggesting Kambli was battling serious medical issues and remained at risk of a brain stroke.

Frustrated Hardik Pandya slams teammates after embarrassing loss vs Punjab Kings MI vs PBKS: Mumbai Indians captain Hardik Pandya cut a frustrated figure after the team's loss against Punjab Kings at the Wankhede Stadium. Asked what went wrong in the game, Hardik said they were outplayed by the visiting team on Thursday, April 16.

Prabhsimran-powered Punjab stay unbeaten, hand Mumbai a reality check Prabhsimran's 80 and a captain's knock from chase master Shreyas Iyer was enough for Punjab Kings to remain unbeaten in the IPL 2026 campaign and pile on the misery on Mumbai Indians at the Wankhede Stadium.

Rs 1cr bargain: Quinton de Kock hits 53-ball ton in timely reminder to MI coaches IPL 2026, MI vs PBKS: Getting his first chance of the season after an injury to Rohit Sharma, Quinton de Kock proved he is not suited for warming the benches. The veteran wicketkeeper-batter smashed 112 off 60 balls, making an immediate impact for the Mumbai Indians.

Manchester City captain Bernardo Silva to leave the club at end of season Manchester City captain Bernardo Silva will depart the club at the end of the season after the expiry of his contract. Bernardo has won 19 major honours with City, including a Champions League title.

Shreyas Iyer defies gravity for relay catch in Mumbai, Rohit's reaction goes viral Shreyas Iyer stunned the Mumbai crowd on Thursday, April 16, pulling off a ridiculous save at the boundary line. Shreyas' gravity defying effort resulted in Hardik Pandya's dismissal in Mumbai Indians vs Punjab Kings.

MS Dhoni sparks frenzy in Hyderabad, fans throng airport for a glimpse. Video MS Dhoni received a rousing welcome in Hyderabad as he travelled for his first away game of IPL 2026. The Chennai Super Kings star's wife shared videos of fan frenzy at the Hyderabad airport on Instagram on Thursday.

Jay Shah named in prestigious Young Global Leaders by World Economic ForumJay Shah has been named in the Young Global Leaders list by the World Economic Forum. The forum recognised Shah for his growing influence in global cricket and his role in expanding the sport through the International Cricket Council.

MI vs PBKS: Suryakumar fails again as India captaincy rumours continue to swirlMI vs PBKS, IPL 2026: Suryakumar Yadav was dismissed for a golden duck by Arshdeep Singh in Mumbai on Thursday. The T20 World Cup-winning captain has struggled for consistency in the new season of the IPL.

Huge blow for MI: Injured Rohit sidelined for more than one game in IPL 2026Hardik Pandya has revealed that Rohit Sharma will be out for at least a couple of games as he failed to recover in time for the clash against PBKS.

FIFA hits back at USA for asking world body to subsidise travel costs for fansFIFA has hit back at the United States after local authorities asked the governing body to subsidise fan travel costs for the FIFA World Cup 2026. Tension has been rising over reports that matchday transport could cost close to $100.

In IPL exile, Lalit Modi places his bet on oldest sport in the worldIPL founder Lalit Modi is working on his next big idea, the former cricket administrator revealed in a chat with former England captain Michael Vaughan. Modi explained his vision of creating a new sporting league, which he believed would find guaranteed success all over the world.

R Ashwin backs ex-RCB pacer to be CSK's replacement for Khaleel AhmedR Ashwin has endorsed former RCB pacer Umesh Yadav as CSK's replacement for Khaleel Ahmed in the ongoing IPL 2026 season. Khaleel was injured during the KKR win and will miss the remainder of the IPL 2026 campaign.

MI vs PBKS Highlights: Punjab Kings thrash Mumbai Indians by 7 wicketsMumbai (MI) vs Punjab (PBKS) Highlights, IPL 2026: PBKS (198/3 in 16.3 ovs). Shreyas Iyer and Prabhsimran Singh have guided Punjab Kings to a terrific victory over Mumbai Indians at the Wankhede Stadium. With the win, Punjab remain unbeaten in the tournament.

After Cummins return news, SRH dealt new injury blow as pacer ruled of IPL 2026Sunrisers Hyderabad have been dealt a new injury blow as overseas pacer David Payne has been ruled out of the remainder of the IPL 2026 season. Hyderabad are set to welcome back Pat Cummins into the mix after his back issue was resolved.

Lalit Modi explains why IND vs PAK bilaterals may never happenLalit Modi says political tensions and a "politicised" PCB are key reasons behind the absence of India vs Pakistan bilaterals, adding he does not see a revival happening anytime soon.

MS Dhoni back? CSK great sets off to Hyderabad, raises comeback hopesIPL 2026: After opting not to travel to Guwahati and Bengaluru for their previous away games, MS Dhoni joined the Chennai Super KIngs squad which left for Hyderabad on Thursday. CSK will play SunRisers Hyderabad on Saturday, April 18.

RCB's golden era? Bangar predicts title spree for Royal Challengers BengaluruSanjay Bangar sees RCB entering a golden era after their maiden IPL title, backing the in-form side to build a winning culture and challenge for multiple championships in the coming years.

Messi in legal trouble over missed Argentina friendlyLionel Messi is facing legal trouble over a missed Argentina friendly in the US. A Miami-based promoter claims his absence led to major financial losses under a multi-million dollar deal.

Vinod Kambli's health worsens: Tendulkar joins WhatsApp group raising supportVinod Kambli continues to fight serious health challenges, with memory issues still a concern. As support grows, Sachin Tendulkar and others have stepped in to help through a private initiative.

IPL: MI rope in Punjab all-rounder Krish Bhagat to replace Atharva AnkolekarMumbai Indians sign Krish Bhagat as replacement for injured Atharva Ankolekar in IPL 2026, with the Punjab all-rounder joining at base price as MI look to revive a slow start.

Vaishali's rise to Candidates champion earns Judith Polgar's praiseR Vaishali's stunning run to Women's Candidates title draws praise from Judit and Susan Polgar on X, as the lowest-rated player seals a World Championship spot with a final-round win.
CSK's Khaleel Ahmed ruled out of IPL 2026 due to injuryIPL 2026: Chennai Super Kings have been dealt a big blow for their ongoing season with their pacer, Khaleel Ahmed being ruled out due to injury.

Rasikh always had it: RCB teammate credits pacer's experience for LSG heroicsIPL 2026: Rasikh Salam's four-wicket haul against LSG drew praise, but not surprise, from Jitesh Sharma. The RCB keeper insisted the pacer always had the skillset, with experience and guidance now helping him deliver consistently.

Rohit Sharma fitness doubt adds to MI's worries against rampant PBKSIPL 2026, MI vs PBKS: Mumbai Indians have struggled so far, losing three matches on the trot, and face a tough challenge against the unbeaten Punjab Kings on Thursday at the Wankhede Stadium. Rohit Sharma's injury concern and Jasprit Bumrah's indifferent form have only added to their worries.

CSK writes to BCCI over 'Dosa Idly' song jibe at Chinnaswamy during RCB clashCSK write to BCCI over 'Dosa Idly' song and DJ remarks at Chinnaswamy during IPL 2026 clash vs RCB, raising concerns over match-day conduct and rivalry tensions.

Vaishali's Candidates triumph: A win vs odds that redefines the underdog termLowest-rated in the field, counted out before it began, Vaishali still found a way. This wasn't just a win, it was a grind through chaos, pressure and moments that could have ended it all.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
